violations.finding: Direct bare python3 call without venv activation: `python3 -c` for IS_SUBAGENT detection. pretool-bash-safety.sh uses $PYTHON_BIN (resolved from $HOME/.claude/venv/bin/python first); userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh has no equivalent PYTHON_BIN resolution and always calls bare python3.
violations.recommendation: Add PYTHON_BIN resolution at top of script matching pretool-bash-safety.sh pattern: PYTHON_BIN="${CLAUDE_PYTHON_BIN:-${HOME}/.claude/venv/bin/python}"; [ ! -x "$PYTHON_BIN" ] && PYTHON_BIN="python3". Replace all python3 calls with $PYTHON_BIN.
violations.newness: pre-existing — python3 calls present in commit 28a1e85 (prior to task 20260521-090200). Advisory only per AC-2.6(b) soft fallback.

violations.recommendation: Use $PYTHON_BIN after adding venv resolution block at top of script.

violations.finding: Chinese text in BLOCKED stderr error message: `echo "BLOCKED: 必须使用正常的UI流程创建session，永远不允许使用代码创建session！" >&2`. Per Standard 6 exemption clause (authoring cycle task-id 20260509-153155): verbatim user-binding quotes belong in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only. BLOCKED stderr diagnostic strings must be English with task-id attribution.
violations.recommendation: Replace with English: `echo "BLOCKED: Session creation via API is FORBIDDEN — use the UI flow (task-id: 20260509-153155)" >&2`. Move Chinese verbatim text to docs/dev/ticket-20260509-153155.md if not already there.
violations.newness: pre-existing — present in commit 28a1e85 (line 832 at that time). Advisory only per AC-2.6(b) soft fallback.

violations.finding: Chinese text in comment: `#   /allow --tool rm 删冗余文件             -> literal "rm" + comment`. This is a usage example comment with Chinese text for the comment argument.
violations.recommendation: Replace with English: `#   /allow --tool rm delete-temp-files      -> literal "rm" + comment`
violations.newness: pre-existing — present in commit 28a1e85. Advisory only per AC-2.6(b) soft fallback.

violations.finding: Chinese text in configuration comment field: `"_comment_anthropic": "以下为可选配置（已禁用）"`. settings.json is not a docs/ file; Standard 6 requires all code, comments, and configuration to be in English.
violations.recommendation: Replace with English: `"_comment_anthropic": "Optional configuration below (disabled)"` or remove the comment field entirely.
violations.newness: pre-existing — present in commit 28a1e85. Advisory only per AC-2.6(b) soft fallback.

summary.newness_method: git-diff-baseline: all violations except userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:192 are confirmed pre-existing in commit 28a1e85 (prior to this cycle). Line 192 finding is partially new due to S1 restructuring in task 20260521-090200. Per AC-2.6(b) soft fallback: overlap-only findings are advisory; fully pre-existing findings are also advisory.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec was blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh hook: the PROMPT variable containing 'rm' text in the prompt body triggered the rm-is-forbidden guard (false positive — rm appeared in the violations description, not as an executable command). Error: 'BLOCKED: rm is forbidden — delete files manually or ask the user'. This is a hook false-positive on the codex invocation command itself.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted. Self-review adversarial questions: (1) False positives — Standard 3 findings correct: userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh genuinely lacks PYTHON_BIN resolution present in pretool-bash-safety.sh. (2) Missed violations — Standard 6 violations in settings.json are within scope as changed file even though not a hook script; finding is valid. (3) Severity calibration — all critical per spec (no major/minor). (4) Changed-files scope — Chinese violations are pre-existing per git diff analysis; correctly marked advisory. (5) Wording accuracy — location citations verified against line numbers in current HEAD.
